briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse winter approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for winter with clear water and post frontal conditions.

Johnston: Use a 3/8 to 3/4-ounce football jig in natural coloring like green pumpkin and bounce it off the bottom for cold-water largemouth that are sitting on or close to the bottom.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a 3/8 to 3/4-ounce football jig in natural coloring like green pumpkin and bounce it off the bottom for cold-water largemouth that are sitting on or close to the bottom.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on rock, point, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: In cold-water months, slow drag jigs. Use drift paddles to dial down drift speed and control your presentation for more effective casting and a better hookup ratio. Be methodical to stay in
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on rock, point, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag

KVD: In clear deep lakes with shad kills, fish often suspend. The higher they are in the water column, the more active they are. Fish off main lake points, humps, drops, centers of big bays — not near the bank. Alabama rig with spinners (keeps rig...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
